title: NEURAL SYNC — Operator–Project Alignment Platform (Phase 1 MVP + Data Pipeline & Source Connectors)
problem_statement:
Traditional developer-to-project allocation relies on skill-set matching alone, ignoring behavioral traits, motivation alignment, work-style compatibility, and career trajectory. This produces suboptimal team composition, elevated attrition, burnout, and low developer satisfaction. NEURAL SYNC must replace static skill-matching with a multi-dimensional, AI-driven compatibility engine that: (1) scores developer↔project fit across five dimensions (skills, work style, motivation, timezone, growth potential), (2) generates a human-readable explanation for every match decision via a configurable LLM explanation layer, (3) predicts bench and burnout risk before they materialize, and (4) continuously re-optimizes allocation so that the system never becomes static. A match the system cannot explain is a broken match.

Phase-1 MVP is complete and deployed. This increment adds the Data Pipeline and Source Connectors feature (Task04 §5 Data Pipelines; §3.1 Profile Enrichment): a uniform Connector abstraction ingests developer signals from external sources (HR systems, GitLab, Jira, Slack, CV documents), maps them into the three text channels consumed by the existing enrich_profile transform (cv_text, git_log_text, slack_text), and flows enriched profiles through the existing create-plus-embed pipeline. Profiles derived from external sources go through the same five-dimension matching engine and LLM explanation layer as manually-created profiles — there is no parallel pipeline. GOVERNANCE NOTE: Phase-1 requirements explicitly deferred external-source connectors to Phase 2 and marked CV document parsing as a non-goal; this increment deliberately pulls both forward with explicit product-agent ratification recorded in this document.

in_scope: Developer profile ingestion: structured DeveloperProfile schema (id, skills, experience_years, preferred_stack, work_style vector, motivation_vector, timezone, availability_hours, career_goals, project history)

Project profile ingestion: structured ProjectProfile schema (id, required_skills, team_structure, workload_intensity, innovation_level, timezone_overlap_required, duration_weeks, growth_opportunities)
Five-dimension matching engine: MATCH_SCORE = w1·skill_score + w2·workstyle_score + w3·motivation_score + w4·timezone_score + w5·growth_score with per-deployment configurable weights
AI explanation layer (configurable LLM provider, Phase-1 Gemini): per-match natural-language output covering match rationale, identified risks, and growth potential
Bench-risk prediction: risk score per developer based on project end dates and allocation schedule
Burnout-risk detection: risk score derived from over-allocation signals, workload intensity, and motivation alignment
REST API (FastAPI) with OpenAPI 3.1 contracts for all resources
PostgreSQL for structured profile and match-history storage
Vector similarity search for behavioral and skill embeddings (embedding store choice resolved in architecture phase)
Developer dashboard view: ranked recommended projects with match scores and LLM-generated explanations
Manager dashboard view: team composition health, risk alerts, allocation suggestions
Match feedback endpoint: developer accepts/rejects a recommendation; rejection rate queryable per developer
GDPR right-to-erasure: full profile and embedding deletion on request
LLM prompt definitions stored as versioned, auditable artifacts
Test suite covering at least one good-match scenario and one bad-match scenario with documented expected score ranges

GOVERNANCE RATIFICATION: Phase-1 requirements marked external-source connectors (Git, Jira, Slack, HR) and CV document parsing as out-of-scope Phase-2 items; this increment deliberately pulls them forward with explicit product-agent ratification; the scope change is recorded here and must not be contradicted in downstream artifacts without a further product-agent revision

Uniform Connector abstraction: a base class that yields SourceDocument records; each SourceDocument carries external_id, display_name, email, cv_text, git_log_text, slack_text, and optional timezone, availability_hours, experience_years, source; every connector must degrade gracefully offline and without credentials, never raising HTTP 5xx

GitLab connector (live, read-only, network kind): given a username and optional project, pulls commit messages and merge-request titles into git_log_text via httpx (not python-gitlab); supports optional token and configurable base_url (default https://gitlab.com); respects a per-request timeout and a configurable page cap (GITLAB_MAX_PAGES); missing or invalid token returns a degraded result, not an error

HR connector (file-based, bulk, file kind): parses a CSV or JSON file into one SourceDocument per employee row; case-insensitive and tolerant column mapping (name, email, title/role/bio into cv_text, skills appended, timezone, weekly_hours into availability_hours, years_experience into experience_years)
Slack connector (file-based, file kind): parses a Slack export JSON file; aggregates each user's messages into slack_text; yields one SourceDocument per user found in the export

CV connector (file-based, file kind): parses a single .txt or .md file into one SourceDocument (cv_text); PDF support is optional and guarded by a conditional import; if the PDF library is unavailable the connector reports it as unavailable rather than raising an exception

Jira connector (credential-gated, network kind): reads assigned issues, labels, and comments into git_log_text via a thin read-only httpx client; exposes a clear interface documenting required credentials (base_url, email, token, project_key); missing credentials return a degraded IngestionSummary documenting what is required, never HTTP 5xx

ETL orchestrator and ingestion endpoints under the /api/v1/ingestion prefix, restricted to the manager role: GET /ingestion/connectors (list connectors with kind and availability), POST /ingestion/file (multipart: file + source + mode), POST /ingestion/gitlab (JSON body: username, project, optional base_url, optional token, mode), POST /ingestion/jira (JSON body: base_url, email, token, project_key, usernames, mode)

Two ingestion modes: preview (returns IngestionSummary with drafts for human review, creates nothing) and commit (creates DeveloperProfile rows via the shared create-plus-embed helper and enqueues embeddings asynchronously)
IngestionSummary response shape: extracted (int), enriched (int), skipped (int), created (int), provenance ({llm: int, heuristic: int}), errors (list of strings)

Shared create-plus-embed helper refactored from the existing create_developer plus _enqueue_embeddings code in src/api/developers.py; both POST /api/v1/developers and commit-mode ingestion must use this single helper with no code duplication
File uploads processed entirely in memory with a configurable size guard (MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES, default 10 MB); uploads exceeding the limit return HTTP 413; nothing is written to disk
Configurable batch record cap (INGESTION_MAX_RECORDS, default 500) to prevent runaway ingestion jobs

Frontend Ingestion page wired into the existing manager-role tab pattern (no react-router): connector picker driven by GET /ingestion/connectors with credential-gated connectors shown disabled and a tooltip stating required credentials; file dropzone for file-kind connectors; GitLab and Jira input forms; Preview action rendering returned drafts in a review table; Approve and Create action re-running in commit mode; multipart-capable request helper added to frontend/src/api/client.ts; inline-style and data-testid conventions matching the existing codebase

New environment variables documented in .env.example: GITLAB_BASE_URL (default https://gitlab.com), GITLAB_TOKEN (default empty), JIRA_BASE_URL (default empty), JIRA_EMAIL (default empty), JIRA_TOKEN (default empty), MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES (default 10485760), INGESTION_MAX_RECORDS (default 500), GITLAB_MAX_PAGES (default 10)
Updated artifacts/api-contracts.json with OpenAPI 3.x path definitions for all ingestion endpoints and ConnectorInfo/IngestionSummary schema components

Updated artifacts/architecture.json with a data-ingestion-service component listing connector subcomponents (gitlab-connector, hr-connector, slack-connector, cv-connector, jira-connector) and documented failure modes (rate-limit, missing-credentials, oversized-upload) each with graceful degradation behavior
out_of_scope: Admin weight-tuning UI panel (weights configurable via API configuration endpoint only in Phase 1)
Real-time streaming reallocation (batch re-optimization is sufficient for Phase 1)
SSO / external identity-provider integration (auth model determined by open question resolution)
Production-scale vector DB managed service deployment (architecture phase decides embedded vs managed)

non_goals: Replacing human management decisions — the system advises, humans decide
Optimizing for short-term project throughput at the expense of developer wellbeing
Functioning as a generic HR management or payroll system
Exposing raw behavioral vectors or motivation scores directly to unauthorized UI consumers
Achieving perfect allocation autonomously without a feedback loop from developers
constraints: Every match response MUST include a non-empty LLM-generated natural-language explanation; skill-only scoring without the behavioral and explainability layer is a hard failure condition
Static allocation without re-optimization capability is a hard failure condition; the system must expose at least a re-score/re-rank endpoint that can be triggered on schedule or on demand
Match recommendation rejection rate MUST be measurable from day 1; rejection feedback collection is not optional
GDPR: all PII must be erasable on request; no data retained beyond consent scope; embeddings must be purged alongside structured records
Match API end-to-end latency MUST be ≤ 500ms at p95 (including or excluding LLM call to be confirmed in open questions; async explanation is acceptable if score + stub are returned synchronously)
System must be horizontally scalable to a developer pool of 10,000+ without architectural rework

Claude Code agents are the mandated agentic build layer (Task04 §3 'CLAUDE CODE AGENT INTEGRATION'), satisfied by the multi-agent SDLC pipeline that produced this system. The runtime explanation LLM is provider-agnostic and configurable: the Phase-1 implementation uses Google Gemini (gemini-1.5-flash, free tier); Claude API (claude-3-5-haiku) is a supported alternate. The model identifier lives in the versioned prompt artifact, so the provider/model is swappable with no source change. Prompts must be versioned and stored as auditable artifacts, not hardcoded strings.

Tech stack is constrained to: Python/FastAPI (backend), React (frontend), a configurable LLM explanation provider (Phase-1: Google Gemini; Claude API supported), PostgreSQL (relational store), vector-compatible storage (technology resolved in architecture phase)
Matching weights w1–w5 must be configurable without a code deployment (via API or config file)

LLM provider for the data pipeline enrichment path must remain Google Gemini; the Anthropic SDK must not be introduced; the provider/model remains swappable via the versioned prompt artifact with no source change required

All ingestion endpoints (/api/v1/ingestion/*) are restricted to the manager role; developer-role JWTs must receive HTTP 403 and unauthenticated requests must receive HTTP 401; the existing require_manager dependency from src/core/auth.py must be reused

Every connector must degrade gracefully when credentials are absent, invalid, or when the external service is unreachable: a missing or bad GitLab/Jira token must produce a degraded IngestionSummary (partial or empty result with an entry in errors) with HTTP 200; no connector failure may produce HTTP 5xx
File uploads must be processed entirely in memory; writing uploads to disk is prohibited; requests with a body exceeding MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES must be rejected with HTTP 413 before any parsing begins

Batch ingestion must skip individual records that yield no extractable skills (counted in IngestionSummary.skipped) without aborting the entire batch; a single unparseable record must not cause HTTP 5xx for the rest of the batch

All new tests must be fully mocked and offline: GitLab and Jira HTTP interactions must use a mock httpx transport; file connectors must be tested from in-memory bytes; all 120 existing tests must remain green after the increment is applied
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1/matches with a valid DeveloperProfile JSON and ProjectProfile JSON returns a response containing match_score (float 0.0–1.0), explanation (non-empty string ≥ 50 chars), risks (list, may be empty), and growth_potential (list, may be empty) within 500ms at p95 under nominal load

Given two profile pairs where Pair A has identical skills but opposing work_style vectors compared to Pair B (aligned work_style), the match_score for Pair A is strictly lower than for Pair B, confirming the behavioral dimension is active and weighted

The LLM-generated explanation for any match includes at minimum: one statement about skill alignment, one statement about work-style or behavioral alignment, and one statement about growth or career potential
GET /api/v1/developers/{id}/risk for a developer with ≥ 48 consecutive weeks of allocation at workload_intensity ≥ 0.8 returns burnout_risk_score > 0.6
GET /api/v1/developers/{id}/risk for a developer whose current project end_date is within 28 days and has no follow-on allocation returns bench_risk_score > 0.7

PUT /api/v1/config/weights with a valid weight map causes subsequent POST /api/v1/matches calls on identical profile pairs to return match_scores that reflect the new weights, verified by a deterministic unit test with known inputs and expected score deltas

The Developer dashboard view renders ≥ 1 recommended project cards (each showing match_score and LLM-generated explanation) for any developer who has a complete profile with all required fields populated
The Manager dashboard view displays per-developer risk badges (bench, burnout) and team composition health without exposing raw behavioral vectors or motivation scalar values to the UI layer

DELETE /api/v1/developers/{id} purges the developer's structured profile, all associated vector embeddings, and all match history records, returning HTTP 204; a subsequent GET /api/v1/developers/{id} returns HTTP 404

POST /api/v1/matches/feedback with developer_id, match_id, and accepted (boolean) stores the feedback record; GET /api/v1/analytics/rejection-rate?developer_id={id} returns the rejection ratio computed from stored feedback with ≥ 1 sample

The automated test suite contains a labeled 'good match' test (high skill + work_style + motivation alignment) where match_score ≥ 0.75, and a labeled 'bad match' test (skill overlap but behavioral mismatch) where match_score ≤ 0.45, with both expected ranges documented in test_plan.json

All LLM prompt templates are stored in a versioned artifact file (not inline code); code_spec.json references the prompt artifact; changing a prompt template does not require modifying application source files
An automated integration test exercises the full match pipeline (profile ingest → embed → score → LLM explain → response) against a local or mocked environment and passes in CI

GET /api/v1/ingestion/connectors returns HTTP 200 with a JSON array containing at least 5 connector descriptors (gitlab, hr, slack, cv, jira); each descriptor includes at minimum the fields kind ('file' or 'network') and availability ('live' or 'credential-gated'); the endpoint is accessible to any authenticated manager-role JWT and returns HTTP 401/403 for other callers

POST /api/v1/ingestion/file with source=cv, a valid UTF-8 .txt body, and mode=preview returns HTTP 200 with an IngestionSummary containing at least one draft entry in a drafts list; IngestionSummary.created equals 0; no DeveloperProfile row is persisted

POST /api/v1/ingestion/file with source=cv, a valid UTF-8 .txt body, and mode=commit returns HTTP 200 with IngestionSummary.created ≥ 1; a DeveloperProfile record is persisted through the shared create-plus-embed helper and embeddings are enqueued asynchronously via BackgroundTasks

POST /api/v1/ingestion/file with source=hr and a valid CSV payload returns one draft per employee row in the response; column mapping is case-insensitive and handles alternate column names (title/role/bio → cv_text; weekly_hours → availability_hours; years_experience → experience_years)
POST /api/v1/ingestion/file with source=slack and a valid Slack export JSON payload returns one draft per user found in the export, with that user's channel messages concatenated as slack_text

POST /api/v1/ingestion/gitlab with a valid username and a mock httpx transport returning commit and MR data returns HTTP 200 with an IngestionSummary whose draft's git_log_text contains the mocked commit messages and MR titles; when called without a token (or with an invalid token), returns HTTP 200 with a degraded (empty or partial) IngestionSummary, never HTTP 5xx

POST /api/v1/ingestion/jira when called with missing or invalid credentials returns HTTP 200 with an IngestionSummary where the errors list contains a message documenting the missing or invalid credential; the endpoint must never return HTTP 5xx regardless of credential state

All /api/v1/ingestion/* endpoints return HTTP 403 when called with a developer-role JWT and HTTP 401 when called without any authentication token; manager-role JWTs are accepted for all ingestion operations

POST /api/v1/ingestion/file with a file body exceeding MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES (configurable, default 10 MB) returns HTTP 413 with a structured error body; no data is written to disk and no DeveloperProfile is created

In mode=commit, the ingestion orchestrator calls enrich_profile(cv_text, git_log_text, slack_text) for each extracted SourceDocument off the event loop (via asyncio.to_thread) and creates DeveloperProfile records through the shared create-plus-embed helper that is also used by POST /api/v1/developers; no separate code path for profile creation exists

When enrich_profile returns a result with an empty skills list for a given SourceDocument, that record is counted in IngestionSummary.skipped and no DeveloperProfile row is created for it; all other records in the same batch continue processing and the HTTP response is still 200

The IngestionSummary response includes a provenance object with separate llm and heuristic integer counts derived from EnrichmentResult.provenance for each enriched record; the sum provenance.llm + provenance.heuristic equals IngestionSummary.enriched

The frontend Ingestion page (rendered only for manager-role sessions, wired into the existing tab pattern without react-router) renders: a connector picker populated from GET /api/v1/ingestion/connectors showing credential-gated connectors as disabled with a tooltip stating required credentials; a file dropzone accepting CSV, JSON, TXT, MD files for file-kind connectors; input forms for GitLab (username, project, optional token) and Jira (base_url, email, token, project_key, usernames); a Preview button that posts in mode=preview and renders returned drafts in a scrollable review table; an Approve and Create button that re-posts in mode=commit; all interactive elements carry data-testid attributes following the existing codebase pattern

An end-to-end ingestion pipeline test in tests/integration/ covers the complete flow: HR CSV connector extraction yields one SourceDocument per row → enrich_profile is called for each document (mocked to return a deterministic EnrichmentResult) → commit mode persists DeveloperProfile rows (verified via MockAsyncSession.added) → embeddings are enqueued (verified via BackgroundTasks mock); all HTTP calls use a mock httpx transport; no live database or external API connections are required; the test passes in CI alongside all 120 existing tests which remain green

src/core/settings.py and .env.example document all new environment variables with safe defaults: GITLAB_BASE_URL (default https://gitlab.com), GITLAB_TOKEN (default empty string), JIRA_BASE_URL (default empty string), JIRA_EMAIL (default empty string), JIRA_TOKEN (default empty string), MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES (default 10485760), INGESTION_MAX_RECORDS (default 500), GITLAB_MAX_PAGES (default 10)

artifacts/api-contracts.json is updated with OpenAPI 3.x path item definitions for GET /api/v1/ingestion/connectors, POST /api/v1/ingestion/file, POST /api/v1/ingestion/gitlab, and POST /api/v1/ingestion/jira, plus ConnectorInfo and IngestionSummary schema components in the components/schemas section

artifacts/architecture.json is updated with a data-ingestion-service component that lists five connector subcomponents (gitlab-connector, hr-connector, slack-connector, cv-connector, jira-connector) and documents failure modes (rate-limit, missing-credentials, oversized-upload) for each, with a degradation field describing the graceful fallback behavior for every failure mode
risks: LLM API latency: synchronous explanation calls may push match response time above 500ms; mitigation: return algorithmic score synchronously and generate explanation asynchronously with caching for repeated profile pairs

Weight misconfiguration: subjective default weights may produce counterintuitive matches and erode developer trust early; mitigation: ship default weights validated against the good-match and bad-match test scenarios defined in acceptance criteria

GDPR scope creep: Phase 2 external-source integration (Slack, Git) introduces sensitive PII before erasure patterns are hardened; mitigation: erasure pipeline must be production-ready in Phase 1 before external sources are added

Sparse rejection-rate signal: at MVP scale the 50% rejection threshold may be statistically unreliable; mitigation: enforce a minimum sample floor of ≥ 10 feedback records before surfacing the rejection-rate alert to managers

Vector search cold-start: embedding similarity quality degrades with very small datasets; mitigation: include a synthetic seed dataset of ≥ 50 developer and ≥ 20 project profiles for development and testing

LLM prompt drift: model version upgrades can silently alter explanation tone or structure; mitigation: snapshot expected explanation structure in the test suite and add a regression check on format (presence of skill, behavioral, and growth sentences)

Behavioral vector subjectivity: self-reported work_style and motivation values may be inaccurate, reducing match quality; mitigation: flag profiles where behavioral vectors are self-reported vs. inferred, and document this limitation in the system

GitLab/Jira rate-limiting: batch ingestion of large teams could exhaust per-token API rate limits mid-batch, producing a partial batch result; mitigation: enforce GITLAB_MAX_PAGES (default 10) and a per-request httpx timeout; log any partial results in IngestionSummary.errors without aborting the batch or returning HTTP 5xx

Large file memory pressure: parsing multi-megabyte CSV or Slack export files entirely in memory under concurrent ingestion requests could cause OOM; mitigation: enforce MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES (default 10 MB) and reject oversized uploads with HTTP 413 before any parsing begins; document the limit in .env.example

Duplicate profile creation: successive commit runs on the same source data may create multiple DeveloperProfile rows for the same individual (same email or external_id) before idempotency semantics are defined; mitigation: resolve commit-mode idempotency in open questions before activating commit mode in production; interim mitigation is to surface duplicate errors in IngestionSummary.errors

Scope increment complexity: pulling external-source connectors and CV parsing from Phase 2 into the current increment increases integration surface area against 120 existing passing tests; mitigation: all new tests are fully mocked and offline; the shared create-plus-embed helper ensures the existing POST /api/v1/developers code path is not duplicated; the reviewer-agent must verify the existing test suite remains green before approving
open_questions: Vector storage: should Phase 1 use an embedded solution (pgvector on PostgreSQL, or Chroma in-process) to minimize operational overhead, or a managed service (Pinecone, Weaviate) for future scalability? Decision blocks DB schema design.

Latency SLA scope: is the 500ms p95 target measured end-to-end including the LLM API call, or only for the algorithmic scoring step (with async explanation delivery acceptable)? Determines whether synchronous LLM calls are permissible.

Authentication model: does Phase 1 require user login (session-based or JWT) for the developer and manager dashboard views, or is API-key-based access sufficient for MVP? Affects frontend and API design.
Seed data availability: are there existing developer or project profiles available for testing, or must the team generate a fully synthetic dataset? Determines test-data pipeline scope.

LLM provider/model selection: Phase-1 uses Google Gemini (gemini-1.5-flash, free tier). Claude API (claude-3-5-haiku) is a supported alternate. The model identifier is stored in the versioned prompt artifact, enabling provider/model swaps with no source change.
Dashboard visual theme: is the cyberpunk aesthetic required for the Phase 1 dashboard, or is a functional but unstyled UI acceptable for MVP with theming deferred to Phase 2?

Commit-mode idempotency: when a SourceDocument's email or external_id matches an existing DeveloperProfile, should commit mode (a) update the existing profile in-place, (b) skip the record and note it in IngestionSummary.errors, or (c) create a second profile? Decision blocks the ingestion DB write path and has GDPR implications (an update path must also cascade to embeddings). Blocks ingestion implementation.

PDF CV support activation: should the optional PDF parsing capability (guarded by a conditional PyMuPDF/pdfminer import) be promoted to a first-class supported feature in a follow-on increment via an explicit PDF_CV_ENABLED environment flag, or remain permanently optional-import-guarded with a clear 'unavailable' message when the library is absent?
